calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Pos-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Of Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions And Declaration Of Stephen M. Kappos In Support Of Motion To Compel Answers To Pos-Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-27-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc's motion to compel responses to post judgment discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses to interrogatories and request for production of documents within 21 days of notice of this order. Failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $340 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2006 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up In Scd.
Off Calendar May-16-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Pappina Thompson.
Elizabeth MacDonald, Esq. for Plaintiff: Creditors TradeAssociation, Inc., dba Collection Bureau.
Plaintiff's Witness: Patrick Brown, present, was sworn and direct examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant: Warren Hinckle, ind., dba Argonaut Press as follows:
Damages $34,296.00
Prejudgment Interest $1,776.60
No Attorney's Fees.
Costs $335.50, for a total of $36,408.10
Judgment signed in open court.
